As shown in Table 4 , both approaches improve upon the base model across all evaluation metrics, confirming the robustness of model soups in enhancing generalization. Uniform soup achieves the best overall results, with 72.36% accuracy and 69.28% F1-score, outperforming greedy soup by +0.13% in accuracy and +0.23% in F1. It also shows slightly higher precision and recall. This suggests that when checkpoint diversity is sufficient, uniform averaging can yield strong and stable performance gains. Greedy soup, while slightly behind in absolute performance, still improves over the base CoAtNet-2 by +0.80% in accuracy and +0.47% in F1-score. Unlike uniform soup, the greedy strategy incrementally selects checkpoints based on validation performance, resulting in a leaner and more selective ensemble. This can be advantageous in scenarios with resource constraints or limited checkpoint quality. Figure 3 illustrates the accuracy of the individual models (CoAtNet-2) and the ensemble models generated via model soups techniques, including greedy soup and uniform soup. It is evident that the individual CoAtNet-2 models consistently yield lower accuracy compared to the model soups ensembles. Interestingly, on the smaller CoAtNet-0 model, the performance gap narrows: uniform soup gains +0.40% in F1-score, while greedy soup yields a slightly higher improvement of +0.44%, despite lower precision in both cases. This variation highlights that the effectiveness of each ensembling strategy may depend on model capacity and the diversity of saved checkpoints. Overall, while uniform soup offers the best results in our setting, greedy soup remains a competitive alternative, especially when computational constraints or checkpoint sparsity are present. Figure 3: Comparison of validation and test accuracy (%) between individual CoAtNet-2 models and their combinations via model soups techniques. The ensemble models – greedy soup and uniform soup – outperform all individual models, demonstrating the effectiveness of model soups in enhancing generalization 4.4.3 Per-Class Performance Analysis To further understand the behavior of our model, we conduct a per-class analysis of CoAtNet-2 in both its best individual checkpoint and the model soups variant, as shown in Table 5 . The results indicate that model soups yields performance improvements in the majority of classes across all evaluation metrics. Notably, model soups enhances classification accuracy in 7 out of 17 classes and improves F1-score in 11 classes. For example, class 6 ( Lễ hội Ok Om Bok của ngư`ơi Khmer ) sees accuracy rise from 61.70% to 74.47% and F1-score from 59.79% to 69.31%, suggesting that ensemble averaging stabilizes predictions in complex or ambiguous categories. Similar improvements are observed in classes 4 ( Lễ hội cúng biển Mỹ Long ), 7 ( Lễ hội Vía Bà Chúa X´ư núi Sam ), 10 ( Lễ làm chay ), and 13 ( Lễ cúng Việc ), which benefit from consistent gains in both precision and recall. In some cases, performance remains unchanged (e.g., classes 1, 2, 5, 11, 12, 14, 15, and 17), or shows slight drops in certain metrics. For instance, class 8 ( Đại lễ Kỳ yên Đình Tân Phư´ơc Tây ) exhibits a drop in accuracy and recall, though precision slightly increases. Similarly, class 16 ( Lễ hội anh hùng Trương ) shows a modest increase in accuracy but a slight decrease in precision. These variations may stem from overfitting in some checkpoints or limited representation during soup selection. Overall, in terms of F1-score, model soups improves or maintains performance in 12 out of 17 classes, reinforcing its robustness and reliability across diverse cultural heritage categories with varying visual complexity and sample sizes. This per-class evaluation confirms that weight-space ensembling not only improves average-case metrics but also contributes to more stable and generalized class-wise performance. 5 Analytically Comparing Model Soups to Soft Voting Given a dataset 𝒟 = { x 1 , x 2 , … , x N } \mathcal{D}=\{x_{1},x_{2},\dots,x_{N}\} of N N samples, each sample is passed through a model f f , followed by a softmax layer, resulting in a probability vector 𝐩 i ( f ) ∈ Δ C − 1 \mathbf{p}^{(f)}_{i}\in\Delta^{C-1} for each input x i x_{i} , where Δ C − 1 \Delta^{C-1} denotes the ( C − 1 ) (C{-}1) -dimensional probability simplex. Collectively, the model f f produces a matrix of softmax outputs: 𝐏 ( f ) = [ ( 𝐩 1 ( f ) ) T ( 𝐩 2 ( f ) ) T ⋮ ( 𝐩 N ( f ) ) T ] ∈ ℝ N × C \mathbf{P}^{(f)}=\begin{bmatrix}(\mathbf{p}^{(f)}_{1})^{T}\\ (\mathbf{p}^{(f)}_{2})^{T}\\ \vdots\\ (\mathbf{p}^{(f)}_{N})^{T}\end{bmatrix}\in\mathbb{R}^{N\times C} To compare two models f f and g g , the mean cross-entropy between their corresponding softmax outputs across the dataset is computed: Dist ​ ( f , g ) \displaystyle\text{Dist}(f,g) = 1 N ​ ∑ i = 1 N H sym ​ ( 𝐩 i ( f ) , 𝐩 i ( g ) ) , \displaystyle=\frac{1}{N}\sum_{i=1}^{N}H_{\text{sym}}\left(\mathbf{p}^{(f)}_{i},\mathbf{p}^{(g)}_{i}\right), (6) where H sym ​ ( 𝐩 , 𝐪 ) \displaystyle\text{where}\quad H_{\text{sym}}(\mathbf{p},\mathbf{q}) = [ H ​ ( 𝐩 , 𝐪 ) + H ​ ( 𝐪 , 𝐩 ) ] , \displaystyle=\left[H(\mathbf{p},\mathbf{q})+H(\mathbf{q},\mathbf{p})\right], H ​ ( 𝐩 , 𝐪 ) \displaystyle H(\mathbf{p},\mathbf{q}) = − ∑ j = 1 C p j ​ log ⁡ q j \displaystyle=-\sum_{j=1}^{C}p_{j}\log q_{j} This yields a symmetric distance matrix D ∈ ℝ | ℱ | × | ℱ | D\in\mathbb{R}^{|\mathcal{F}|\times|\mathcal{F}|} over a set of models ℱ \mathcal{F} . To visualize the relational structure between models, Multidimensional Scaling (MDS) Demaine et al. [ 2021 ] is applied to project the distance matrix D D into a two-dimensional space. MDS is particularly well-suited for this task because pairwise distances are preserved in the low-dimensional embedding, enabling the relative similarity between models to be captured and interpreted based purely on their output behaviors. Unlike other nonlinear techniques such as t-SNE Cai and Ma [ 2022 ] , which may distort global geometry, both local and global distances are maintained by MDS, rendering it more appropriate for preserving structural fidelity in model comparison tasks. Model Soups Diversity Justification The aforementioned pairwise distance matrix D D and its corresponding MDS projection are also utilized as a basis for analyzing model diversity in the context of model soups . Specifically, the 2D embedding space is employed to visualize the distribution of candidate models and to evaluate whether the models selected for ensembling (i.e., those included in the soup) are well-spread in output space. Let 𝒮 ⊂ ℱ \mathcal{S}\subset\mathcal{F} denote the subset of models chosen for model soups . If model soups is effective at capturing a diverse ensemble, the points corresponding to 𝒮 \mathcal{S} in the MDS embedding are expected to exhibit broad coverage of the model space – avoiding clustering and instead spanning distinct regions. In the experiments conducted, the selected models 𝒮 \mathcal{S} are highlighted in the MDS scatter plot and observed to be well-separated, confirming that the soup does not merely average redundant models, but rather aggregates models with heterogeneous prediction behaviors. This diversity in softmax outputs contributes to the ensemble’s robustness and improved generalization. Thus, the analysis provides empirical evidence that the model soups construction process implicitly favors diverse members in the model space, a property known to be beneficial for ensembling in both theory and practice. Visualizing Model Diversity on the Validation Set To evaluate the diversity of models selected by model soups , the softmax outputs (i.e., probabilities) of all candidate and ensemble models are projected using Multidimensional Scaling (MDS) based on pairwise cross-entropy distances. The validation set is chosen for visualization (Figure 4 ), as it reflects the basis on which checkpoint selection is performed by both greedy and uniform soup strategies. The resulting 2D embedding reveals that the ingredient models (blue) are well-dispersed across the output space, indicating a high degree of predictive diversity. In contrast, the ensemble models – Greedy (red), Uniform (green), and Soft Voting (orange) – are observed to be clustered near the center of this distribution. This geometry suggests that model soups draws from a broad range of behaviors and aggregates diverse checkpoints, while Soft Voting averages across more redundant predictions. The geometric separation among the ingredients confirms that model soups does not merely average similar models but actively selects complementary ones. Such diversity is critical for reducing variance in ensemble predictions and improving generalization. This visualization provides compelling evidence that model soups functions as a diversity-aware selection strategy, offering both theoretical and empirical benefits over traditional voting-based ensembles. Figure 4: MDS visualization of model predictions on the validation set. Each point represents a model projected via MDS using cross-entropy-based pairwise distances. The ingredient models (blue) are broadly distributed, while ensemble models – Greedy (red), Uniform (green), and Soft Voting (orange) – cluster near the center. This spatial structure confirms that model soups leverages predictive diversity more effectively than Soft Voting. The value above each point represents the accuracy on the validation set for the corresponding model. In Soft Voting, the ensemble prediction is computed by averaging the output probability vectors from all individual models in the ensemble. Given a set of M M trained models { f ( 1 ) , f ( 2 ) , … , f ( M ) } \{f^{(1)},f^{(2)},\dots,f^{(M)}\} , the ensemble output for an input x x is defined as: 𝐩 soft ​ ( x ) = 1 M ​ ∑ m = 1 M 𝐩 ( f ( m ) ) ​ ( x ) \mathbf{p}^{\text{soft}}(x)=\frac{1}{M}\sum_{m=1}^{M}\mathbf{p}^{(f^{(m)})}(x) where 𝐩 ( f ( m ) ) ​ ( x ) ∈ Δ C − 1 \mathbf{p}^{(f^{(m)})}(x)\in\Delta^{C-1} denotes the softmax output of model f ( m ) f^{(m)} for input x x , and Δ C − 1 \Delta^{C-1} is the ( C − 1 ) (C{-}1) -dimensional probability simplex over C C classes. The final predicted class is then: y ^ = arg max j [ 𝐩 soft ( x ) ] j \hat{y}=\arg\max_{j}\left[\mathbf{p}^{\text{soft}}(x)\right]_{j} This method treats all models as equally informative, regardless of their individual accuracy or diversity, which may limit its effectiveness when model predictions are highly correlated. Comparison with Soft Voting and Bias-Variance Analysis To better understand the role of model soups in improving generalization, it is compared to the classical Soft Voting ensemble. In Soft Voting, all models contribute equally to the final prediction through uniform averaging of their softmax outputs, regardless of their individual performance or redundancy. This approach does not explicitly account for diversity among models, and can lead to ensembling over highly similar checkpoints. As shown in Figure 4 , the Soft Voting ensemble (orange) is located near the center of the output-space embedding, reflecting its averaging over clustered, potentially redundant models. In contrast, model soups – particularly in its greedy and uniform forms – constructs the ensemble by selectively averaging a subset of validation-optimal checkpoints that are geometrically diverse in the output space. This selective diversity is not incidental; from a bias–variance perspective, averaging over complementary models with uncorrelated errors is known to reduce prediction variance while introducing minimal additional bias. The analysis demonstrates that model soups leverages this property effectively, functioning as a variance-reducing, diversity-aware ensemble strategy. Overall, model soups goes beyond naive output averaging: it dynamically adapts to the geometry of model outputs and the validation objective, yielding more robust predictions under low-resource and high-variance conditions compared to Soft Voting. Table 6: Comparison of Model Soups and Soft Voting on key ensemble properties. Aspect Soft Voting Model Soups Inference Cost High (all models active) Low (single averaged model) Memory Footprint Scales with number of models Constant (single model) Deployment Simplicity Requires parallel model storage Simple (one model checkpoint) Bias Reduction Limited Slight increase (depends on soup strategy) Variance Reduction Moderate (correlated models) High (diverse checkpoint averaging) 6 Ablation Study: Effect of Removing Pre-training To further investigate the impact of pre-trained initialization, an ablation study was conducted where both CoAtNet-0 and CoAtNet-2 were trained from random initialization rather than being fine-tuned from ImageNet pre-trained weights. The models were trained under the same experimental settings as in the main experiments, and their performance was evaluated on the ICH-17 test set. Table 7: Performance of CoAtNet models trained from random initialization on the ICH-17 test set. The values located in the upper right corner of the entries in the rows corresponding to uniform soup and greedy soup indicate the difference compared to the corresponding model without applying model soups . Model Accuracy Precision Recall F1 CoAtNet-0 49.13 46.36 45.31 44.85 CoAtNet-2 51.27 48.87 48.21 47.81 CoAtNet-2 + uniform 52.07 ↑ 0.80 52.07^{\uparrow 0.80} 49.27 ↑ 0.40 49.27^{\uparrow 0.40} 48.82 ↑ 0.61 48.82^{\uparrow 0.61} 47.99 ↑ 0.18 47.99^{\uparrow 0.18} CoAtNet-2 + greedy 51.80 ↑ 0.53 51.80^{\uparrow 0.53} 48.72 ↓ 0.15 48.72^{\downarrow 0.15} 48.77 ↑ 0.56 48.77^{\uparrow 0.56} 48.11 ↑ 0.30 48.11^{\uparrow 0.30} As shown in Table 7 , both models trained from scratch perform significantly worse compared to their ImageNet-pretrained counterparts (CoAtNet-0: 70.63% accuracy, 65.98% F1; CoAtNet-2: 71.43% accuracy, 68.58% F1). Training without pre-training results in drops of approximately 20–22 percentage points in accuracy and 21–23 percentage points in macro F1-score. These results highlight that pre-training provides critical inductive biases and transferable representations, which are especially important in the low-resource and noisy ICH-17 dataset. 7 Conclusion and Future Work In this study, we introduced a robust framework for classifying images of Intangible Cultural Heritage (ICH) in the Mekong Delta by combining the hybrid CoAtNet architecture with weight-space ensembling via model soups . By leveraging the representational power of CoAtNet and the generalization benefits of checkpoint averaging, our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on the ICH-17 dataset. We adopted both uniform and greedy soup strategies, outperforming strong baselines such as ResNet-50, DenseNet-121, and ViT across global accuracy and per-class F1-score metrics. Beyond performance gains, a comprehensive analysis of model diversity was conducted using cross-entropy-based distances and Multidimensional Scaling (MDS). This analysis reveals that the models selected by the soup strategy span diverse regions in output space – unlike classical Soft Voting, which averages over tightly clustered models. The findings empirically confirm that model soups acts not merely as an averaging scheme, but as a diversity-aware selection mechanism that enhances robustness and generalization across training, validation, and test splits. For future work, this framework is planned to be extended in two directions. First, integrating semantic priors and multi-modal signals (e.g., textual metadata) will be investigated to further enhance performance in low-resource cultural datasets. Second, the methodology is intended to be scaled to broader ICH datasets across other regions, contributing to inclusive and AI-driven preservation of cultural heritage at both regional and global levels. Acknowledgment We would like to express our sincere gratitude to the AniAge project for providing the ICH dataset used in our experiments. This valuable resource enabled a fair and rigorous evaluation of the proposed methods in a culturally grounded context. Cited by: §4.2 . {appendices} 8 The Method for Selecting the Best Checkpoint in Our Approach Instead of selecting only a single checkpoint, such as the one with the highest accuracy or lowest loss based on the validation set, the top k = 8 k=8 checkpoints for each metric, including loss, accuracy, and F1 score, were saved based on the validation set, resulting in a total of 24 24 best checkpoints saved. These checkpoints were then evaluated on the test set, and the results of the checkpoint with the highest F1 score were reported. In cases where multiple checkpoints had equal F1 scores, they were compared based on accuracy. This approach was adopted because, during the experimental phase, it was observed that some results on the validation set were significantly high, yet a relatively large gap was found on the test set (in some cases, up to a 4% difference in accuracy). This could lead to a scenario where the checkpoint with the highest validation performance is selected, resulting in relatively poor test set performance. We hypothesize that this phenomenon is due to the characteristics of the ICH-17 dataset, where the context of the images is highly diverse, and the data remains relatively noisy, reducing the model’s generalization capability. Furthermore, the number of images per class in both the test and validation sets is limited when considered individually, combined with the diverse contexts of the images, which may lead to an inconsistent distribution between the validation and test sets. However, it should be noted that this does not affect the application of model soups , as evaluation and checkpoint selection were performed entirely based on the validation set results, and the test set was only evaluated on the final checkpoint aggregated from the uniform soup and greedy soup algorithms. sections.summary: 本研究针对湄公河三角洲非物质文化遗产(ICH)图像分类问题提出了一种创新框架，该框架结合了混合CoAtNet架构和模型汤(model soups)技术。研究背景源于ICH图像分类面临的独特挑战：标注数据有限、类别间视觉相似性高以及领域异质性。传统深度学习模型在低资源环境下容易出现高方差或过度拟合虚假相关性，导致泛化能力差。作者采用CoAtNet作为骨干网络，该网络通过阶段式融合卷积和自注意力机制捕获局部和全局模式。核心创新在于应用模型汤技术进行权重空间集成，通过平均单次训练轨迹中的多个检查点来构建集成模型，而不增加推理成本。研究实现了两种集成策略：贪婪汤和均匀汤，分别用于选择性组合不同检查点。实验结果表明，该方法在ICH-17数据集上达到72.36%的top-1准确率和69.28%的宏F1分数，显著超越了ResNet-50、DenseNet-121和ViT等强基线模型。通过偏差-方差分解分析发现，模型汤通过稳定不同模型快照的预测来减少方差，同时引入最小额外偏差。使用交叉熵距离度量和多维缩放(MDS)的分析显示，模型汤选择了几何多样性检查点，而软投票则混合了输出空间中心的冗余模型。
sections.problem-setting: 湄公河三角洲非物质文化遗产图像分类面临多重挑战。首先，数据稀缺性问题严重，整个ICH-17数据集仅有7,406张图像分布在17个类别中，平均每类约435张图像，这在深度学习场景下属于典型的低资源环境。其次，类别间视觉相似性极高，如论文图1所示，第4类(美龙海祭节)和第8类(新福西亭祈安大典)共享高度相似的视觉上下文，使得区分变得极其困难。第三，文化表达形式的多样性导致视觉特征复杂，包括传统音乐表演、手工艺制作、宗教仪式等多种文化实践形式。第四，数据集中存在显著的噪声和无关图像，尽管经过手动后处理，仍包含相当比例的干扰信息。第五，某些类别样本数量极不均衡，从最少的185张(第2类)到最多的642张(第3类)不等。这些因素共同构成了一个高难度的多类别分类任务，传统的单一模型往往在这样的环境中表现不佳，容易出现过拟合或泛化能力不足的问题。研究团队识别出这些问题后，决定探索集成学习方法来提升模型的鲁棒性和泛化性能。
sections.method-breakdown: 方法论的核心由两个主要组件构成：CoAtNet混合架构和模型汤权重空间集成技术。CoAtNet架构采用五阶段设计，其中S0阶段为卷积茎(S0)，由标准卷积层组成；S1和S2阶段采用MBConv块，利用深度可分离卷积和挤压激励机制高效捕获局部特征；S3和S4阶段转换为Transformer块，整合相对自注意力和卷积前馈网络来建模全局依赖关系。这种C-C-T-T配置使CoAtNet能够保持空间泛化的强归纳偏置，同时增强学习长程交互的能力。模型汤技术采用贪心汤程序：首先选择验证准确率最高的检查点，然后仅当添加候选检查点能提高验证性能时才纳入。设{θ(1), θ(2), ..., θ(T)}表示不同epoch保存的检查点集合，贪心选择识别子集S⊆{1,...,T}。最终集成模型(均匀汤)计算为：θsoup = (1/|S|)∑k∈S θ(k)。算法流程包括初始化最佳检查点、遍历所有检查点、计算临时平均值、评估临时准确率、根据性能改进决定是否接受检查点等步骤。这种方法在权重空间内插不同的解决方案，减少过拟合并利用中间模型状态的互补优势。整个过程无需额外联合训练，保持推理效率。
sections.training-inference: 训练路径采用端到端微调策略，所有模型参数均参与微调。训练设置包括：批次大小128，训练50个epoch，使用交叉熵损失函数，AdamW优化器，初始学习率1×10^-4，权重衰减1×10^-3。学习率调度采用带热重启的余弦退火策略，设置min_lr=0.0，周期长度T0=3，乘法因子T_mult=1。梯度范数裁剪阈值设为1.0以确保训练稳定性。所有实验使用fp16混合精度。对于每个指标(loss、accuracy、F1 score)，基于验证集保存top k=8个检查点，总共保存24个最佳检查点。推理路径保持与标准模型相同的计算复杂度，因为模型汤通过权重平均创建单一模型，避免了多模型并行推理的开销。推理时只需加载平均后的权重文件，执行标准前向传播即可。这种设计确保了性能提升的同时不增加推理时间成本，对于实际部署具有重要意义。训练过程中应用了水平翻转、MixUp和CutMix等数据增强技术，图像统一调整为224×224像素并按ImageNet统计量标准化。验证集用于监控训练过程和构建模型汤。
sections.experiment-breakdown: 实验在ICH-17数据集上进行，该数据集包含17个不同类别共7,406张图像。原始数据集分为训练集(6,657张)和测试集(749张)，其中训练集进一步随机划分为新的训练子集(6,057张)和验证子集(600张)。实验评估了多个基线模型：ResNet-50(23.54M参数)、DenseNet-121(6.97M参数)、ViT(85.81M参数)、CoAtNet-0(26.68M参数)和CoAtNet-2(72.86M参数)。所有模型均在ImageNet-1k上预训练后在ICH-17上微调。评估指标包括准确率、精确率、召回率和F1分数，除准确率外其他指标采用宏平均以平衡类别不均衡。实验比较了基础模型与应用模型汤后的性能差异，包括均匀汤和贪心汤两种策略。每种策略保存top-8检查点，基于验证集性能选择最优组合。实验还进行了消融研究，比较了有无预训练的性能差异。MDS可视化用于分析模型多样性，通过交叉熵距离度量模型间的几何分布。软投票方法作为对比基准，用于验证模型汤的多样性选择优势。
sections.result-interpretation: 实验结果显示CoAtNet-2+均匀汤达到最佳性能：72.36%准确率和69.28%宏F1分数，相比基线模型显著提升。CoAtNet-0在没有集成的情况下已优于所有传统基线，证明了混合架构的有效性。模型汤技术在所有模型上都带来性能提升，CoAtNet-2的均匀汤比基线提升0.93%准确率和0.70% F1分数。贪心汤策略也表现出色，虽然略低于均匀汤，但仍明显优于基线。消融研究表明预训练对性能至关重要，从零开始训练的模型性能下降约20-22个百分点。每类性能分析显示，模型汤在17个类别中的12个类别上改善或维持了F1分数。特别是对于视觉相似的困难类别(如第4类和第8类)，模型汤显著提升了分类准确性。MDS可视化证实模型汤选择的检查点在输出空间中几何分布多样，而软投票倾向于平均相似模型。偏差-方差分析表明模型汤通过稳定不同快照的预测有效降低方差，同时引入最小偏差。这些结果证明了权重空间集成在低资源文化图像分类任务中的有效性。
sections.limitations: 研究存在几个明确的技术限制。首先，数据集规模相对较小(7,406张图像)，可能限制了模型的泛化能力，且数据收集主要依赖Google搜索可能存在标注质量不一致的问题。其次，模型汤技术需要保存多个检查点，增加了存储开销，尽管推理成本不变但训练期间的存储需求增加。第三，k=8的选择基于经验验证，缺乏理论指导，不同任务可能需要不同的k值。第四，研究仅限于图像分类任务，未探索其他计算机视觉任务如目标检测或分割的适用性。第五，消融研究显示预训练对性能至关重要，从零开始训练的模型性能大幅下降，说明该方法对预训练模型的依赖性较强。第六，模型汤的贪心选择策略可能导致局部最优，而非全局最优的检查点组合。第七，跨域泛化能力未得到充分验证，仅在特定文化背景的数据集上进行了测试。第八，计算资源限制可能影响了更大规模实验的设计和执行。
sections.reproducibility: 研究提供了详细的可重现性信息。实验在配备AMD EPYC 7702 64核处理器(分配16物理核)、31GB内存和NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 GPU的系统上进行。所有超参数设置明确：批次大小128、训练50轮、学习率1×10^-4、权重衰减1×10^-3、梯度裁剪阈值1.0等。数据预处理包括图像尺寸调整至224×224像素和基于ImageNet统计量的归一化。使用了标准的开源库和预训练模型，如PyTorch和timm库。代码实现基于公开的模型汤算法，提供了具体的实现细节。数据集划分明确：训练集6,057张、验证集600张、测试集749张。评估指标计算方式详细描述，包括宏平均的具体公式。然而，完整源代码的可用性未明确提及，可能影响完全复现实验。预训练模型来自公开仓库，但具体版本和配置需仔细匹配。实验的随机种子设置未详细说明，可能影响结果的精确复现。
sections.ideas-for-my-work: 首先，将模型汤技术应用于我的低资源图像分类任务，特别是在数据稀缺且类别间相似度高的场景下。我将采用论文中的贪心汤算法，保存训练过程中的top-k检查点，并通过验证集性能选择最优组合。这个方法可以在不增加推理成本的前提下提升模型泛化能力，特别适合部署资源受限的应用场景。其次，借鉴CoAtNet的混合架构设计思路，在我的项目中融合卷积和注意力机制，利用卷积的局部特征提取能力和注意力的全局建模能力。我会按照论文的C-C-T-T配置设计网络结构，逐步从卷积块过渡到Transformer块，以平衡局部模式捕捉和长程依赖建模的需求。第三，采用论文中的MDS可视化方法分析我训练的不同模型检查点之间的多样性，通过交叉熵距离度量模型输出的几何分布，确保集成的模型具有互补性而非冗余性。这将帮助我理解模型汤选择的检查点是否真正多样化，从而优化集成策略。
sections.writing-usage: 论文在方法描述中使用了清晰的数学公式和算法伪代码，如模型汤的权重平均公式θsoup = (1/|S|)∑k∈S θ(k)，为技术文档写作提供了良好范例。结果呈现采用了表格对比的方式，将基线模型与改进方法的性能指标并列展示，便于读者快速理解改进效果。实验部分详细说明了数据集划分、超参数设置和评估指标，为可重现性研究提供了标准模板。图表说明简洁明了，如MDS可视化图清晰展示了模型间的关系和多样性。论文在讨论局限性时坦诚面对研究不足，为后续工作指明方向。写作结构遵循引言-相关工作-方法-实验-分析-结论的经典模式，逻辑清晰。技术术语定义准确，如对CoAtNet架构各阶段功能的描述。结果解释结合定量数据和定性分析，增强了说服力。
sections.theory-notes: 模型汤技术基于权重空间集成理论，通过平均不同训练快照的参数来构建更鲁棒的模型。该方法利用了深度学习模型训练轨迹中存在多个局部最优解的现象，通过参数空间的凸组合获得更好的泛化性能。偏差-方差分解理论支持了该方法的有效性：模型汤通过平均不同模型的预测减少方差，同时引入最小偏差。CoAtNet架构体现了卷积归纳偏置与自注意力全局建模能力的结合，前者提供平移不变性和局部特征提取，后者捕获长程依赖关系。多维缩放(MDS)分析基于流形学习理论，通过保持成对距离在低维空间中可视化高维模型输出的几何结构。软投票与模型汤的对比揭示了多样性在集成学习中的重要性：几何多样性的模型组合比相似模型的简单平均更有效。交叉熵距离度量反映了模型输出概率分布间的差异性，为模型多样性量化提供了理论基础。
sections.metadata: 论文来源为arXiv预印本服务器，编号arXiv:2603.02181v1，发表时间为2026年3月2日，作者为Can Tho University的研究人员。论文类型为期刊投稿，接收日期为2025年9月24日，早期访问日期为2025年10月1日。期刊为MIC-ICT Research，卷号2025，期号3。论文可靠性较高，基于已建立的模型汤技术和CoAtNet架构，实验设计相对完整。可复现性等级为中等偏高，提供了详细的实验设置和评估协议，但缺少完整的开源代码。研究主题聚焦于文化遗产保护的计算机视觉应用，属于跨学科研究领域。论文引用了Wortsman等人关于模型汤的开创性工作，以及Dai等人关于CoAtNet的原始论文，理论基础扎实。研究机构为越南芹苴大学信息技术与通信学院，具有相关领域的研究背景。

# Leveraging Model Soups to Classify Intangible Cultural Heritage Images from the Mekong Delta

## Summary

本研究针对湄公河三角洲非物质文化遗产(ICH)图像分类问题提出了一种创新框架，该框架结合了混合CoAtNet架构和模型汤(model soups)技术。研究背景源于ICH图像分类面临的独特挑战：标注数据有限、类别间视觉相似性高以及领域异质性。传统深度学习模型在低资源环境下容易出现高方差或过度拟合虚假相关性，导致泛化能力差。作者采用CoAtNet作为骨干网络，该网络通过阶段式融合卷积和自注意力机制捕获局部和全局模式。核心创新在于应用模型汤技术进行权重空间集成，通过平均单次训练轨迹中的多个检查点来构建集成模型，而不增加推理成本。研究实现了两种集成策略：贪婪汤和均匀汤，分别用于选择性组合不同检查点。实验结果表明，该方法在ICH-17数据集上达到72.36%的top-1准确率和69.28%的宏F1分数，显著超越了ResNet-50、DenseNet-121和ViT等强基线模型。通过偏差-方差分解分析发现，模型汤通过稳定不同模型快照的预测来减少方差，同时引入最小额外偏差。使用交叉熵距离度量和多维缩放(MDS)的分析显示，模型汤选择了几何多样性检查点，而软投票则混合了输出空间中心的冗余模型。

## Method Breakdown

方法论的核心由两个主要组件构成：CoAtNet混合架构和模型汤权重空间集成技术。CoAtNet架构采用五阶段设计，其中S0阶段为卷积茎(S0)，由标准卷积层组成；S1和S2阶段采用MBConv块，利用深度可分离卷积和挤压激励机制高效捕获局部特征；S3和S4阶段转换为Transformer块，整合相对自注意力和卷积前馈网络来建模全局依赖关系。这种C-C-T-T配置使CoAtNet能够保持空间泛化的强归纳偏置，同时增强学习长程交互的能力。模型汤技术采用贪心汤程序：首先选择验证准确率最高的检查点，然后仅当添加候选检查点能提高验证性能时才纳入。设{θ(1), θ(2), ..., θ(T)}表示不同epoch保存的检查点集合，贪心选择识别子集S⊆{1,...,T}。最终集成模型(均匀汤)计算为：θsoup = (1/|S|)∑k∈S θ(k)。算法流程包括初始化最佳检查点、遍历所有检查点、计算临时平均值、评估临时准确率、根据性能改进决定是否接受检查点等步骤。这种方法在权重空间内插不同的解决方案，减少过拟合并利用中间模型状态的互补优势。整个过程无需额外联合训练，保持推理效率。

## Training and Inference

训练路径采用端到端微调策略，所有模型参数均参与微调。训练设置包括：批次大小128，训练50个epoch，使用交叉熵损失函数，AdamW优化器，初始学习率1×10^-4，权重衰减1×10^-3。学习率调度采用带热重启的余弦退火策略，设置min_lr=0.0，周期长度T0=3，乘法因子T_mult=1。梯度范数裁剪阈值设为1.0以确保训练稳定性。所有实验使用fp16混合精度。对于每个指标(loss、accuracy、F1 score)，基于验证集保存top k=8个检查点，总共保存24个最佳检查点。推理路径保持与标准模型相同的计算复杂度，因为模型汤通过权重平均创建单一模型，避免了多模型并行推理的开销。推理时只需加载平均后的权重文件，执行标准前向传播即可。这种设计确保了性能提升的同时不增加推理时间成本，对于实际部署具有重要意义。训练过程中应用了水平翻转、MixUp和CutMix等数据增强技术，图像统一调整为224×224像素并按ImageNet统计量标准化。验证集用于监控训练过程和构建模型汤。

## Experiment Breakdown

实验在ICH-17数据集上进行，该数据集包含17个不同类别共7,406张图像。原始数据集分为训练集(6,657张)和测试集(749张)，其中训练集进一步随机划分为新的训练子集(6,057张)和验证子集(600张)。实验评估了多个基线模型：ResNet-50(23.54M参数)、DenseNet-121(6.97M参数)、ViT(85.81M参数)、CoAtNet-0(26.68M参数)和CoAtNet-2(72.86M参数)。所有模型均在ImageNet-1k上预训练后在ICH-17上微调。评估指标包括准确率、精确率、召回率和F1分数，除准确率外其他指标采用宏平均以平衡类别不均衡。实验比较了基础模型与应用模型汤后的性能差异，包括均匀汤和贪心汤两种策略。每种策略保存top-8检查点，基于验证集性能选择最优组合。实验还进行了消融研究，比较了有无预训练的性能差异。MDS可视化用于分析模型多样性，通过交叉熵距离度量模型间的几何分布。软投票方法作为对比基准，用于验证模型汤的多样性选择优势。

## Result Interpretation

实验结果显示CoAtNet-2+均匀汤达到最佳性能：72.36%准确率和69.28%宏F1分数，相比基线模型显著提升。CoAtNet-0在没有集成的情况下已优于所有传统基线，证明了混合架构的有效性。模型汤技术在所有模型上都带来性能提升，CoAtNet-2的均匀汤比基线提升0.93%准确率和0.70% F1分数。贪心汤策略也表现出色，虽然略低于均匀汤，但仍明显优于基线。消融研究表明预训练对性能至关重要，从零开始训练的模型性能下降约20-22个百分点。每类性能分析显示，模型汤在17个类别中的12个类别上改善或维持了F1分数。特别是对于视觉相似的困难类别(如第4类和第8类)，模型汤显著提升了分类准确性。MDS可视化证实模型汤选择的检查点在输出空间中几何分布多样，而软投票倾向于平均相似模型。偏差-方差分析表明模型汤通过稳定不同快照的预测有效降低方差，同时引入最小偏差。这些结果证明了权重空间集成在低资源文化图像分类任务中的有效性。

## Limitations

研究存在几个明确的技术限制。首先，数据集规模相对较小(7,406张图像)，可能限制了模型的泛化能力，且数据收集主要依赖Google搜索可能存在标注质量不一致的问题。其次，模型汤技术需要保存多个检查点，增加了存储开销，尽管推理成本不变但训练期间的存储需求增加。第三，k=8的选择基于经验验证，缺乏理论指导，不同任务可能需要不同的k值。第四，研究仅限于图像分类任务，未探索其他计算机视觉任务如目标检测或分割的适用性。第五，消融研究显示预训练对性能至关重要，从零开始训练的模型性能大幅下降，说明该方法对预训练模型的依赖性较强。第六，模型汤的贪心选择策略可能导致局部最优，而非全局最优的检查点组合。第七，跨域泛化能力未得到充分验证，仅在特定文化背景的数据集上进行了测试。第八，计算资源限制可能影响了更大规模实验的设计和执行。

## Reproducibility

研究提供了详细的可重现性信息。实验在配备AMD EPYC 7702 64核处理器(分配16物理核)、31GB内存和NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 GPU的系统上进行。所有超参数设置明确：批次大小128、训练50轮、学习率1×10^-4、权重衰减1×10^-3、梯度裁剪阈值1.0等。数据预处理包括图像尺寸调整至224×224像素和基于ImageNet统计量的归一化。使用了标准的开源库和预训练模型，如PyTorch和timm库。代码实现基于公开的模型汤算法，提供了具体的实现细节。数据集划分明确：训练集6,057张、验证集600张、测试集749张。评估指标计算方式详细描述，包括宏平均的具体公式。然而，完整源代码的可用性未明确提及，可能影响完全复现实验。预训练模型来自公开仓库，但具体版本和配置需仔细匹配。实验的随机种子设置未详细说明，可能影响结果的精确复现。

## Ideas for My Work

首先，将模型汤技术应用于我的低资源图像分类任务，特别是在数据稀缺且类别间相似度高的场景下。我将采用论文中的贪心汤算法，保存训练过程中的top-k检查点，并通过验证集性能选择最优组合。这个方法可以在不增加推理成本的前提下提升模型泛化能力，特别适合部署资源受限的应用场景。其次，借鉴CoAtNet的混合架构设计思路，在我的项目中融合卷积和注意力机制，利用卷积的局部特征提取能力和注意力的全局建模能力。我会按照论文的C-C-T-T配置设计网络结构，逐步从卷积块过渡到Transformer块，以平衡局部模式捕捉和长程依赖建模的需求。第三，采用论文中的MDS可视化方法分析我训练的不同模型检查点之间的多样性，通过交叉熵距离度量模型输出的几何分布，确保集成的模型具有互补性而非冗余性。这将帮助我理解模型汤选择的检查点是否真正多样化，从而优化集成策略。

## Writing Usage

论文在方法描述中使用了清晰的数学公式和算法伪代码，如模型汤的权重平均公式θsoup = (1/|S|)∑k∈S θ(k)，为技术文档写作提供了良好范例。结果呈现采用了表格对比的方式，将基线模型与改进方法的性能指标并列展示，便于读者快速理解改进效果。实验部分详细说明了数据集划分、超参数设置和评估指标，为可重现性研究提供了标准模板。图表说明简洁明了，如MDS可视化图清晰展示了模型间的关系和多样性。论文在讨论局限性时坦诚面对研究不足，为后续工作指明方向。写作结构遵循引言-相关工作-方法-实验-分析-结论的经典模式，逻辑清晰。技术术语定义准确，如对CoAtNet架构各阶段功能的描述。结果解释结合定量数据和定性分析，增强了说服力。

## Theory Notes

模型汤技术基于权重空间集成理论，通过平均不同训练快照的参数来构建更鲁棒的模型。该方法利用了深度学习模型训练轨迹中存在多个局部最优解的现象，通过参数空间的凸组合获得更好的泛化性能。偏差-方差分解理论支持了该方法的有效性：模型汤通过平均不同模型的预测减少方差，同时引入最小偏差。CoAtNet架构体现了卷积归纳偏置与自注意力全局建模能力的结合，前者提供平移不变性和局部特征提取，后者捕获长程依赖关系。多维缩放(MDS)分析基于流形学习理论，通过保持成对距离在低维空间中可视化高维模型输出的几何结构。软投票与模型汤的对比揭示了多样性在集成学习中的重要性：几何多样性的模型组合比相似模型的简单平均更有效。交叉熵距离度量反映了模型输出概率分布间的差异性，为模型多样性量化提供了理论基础。

## Metadata

论文来源为arXiv预印本服务器，编号arXiv:2603.02181v1，发表时间为2026年3月2日，作者为Can Tho University的研究人员。论文类型为期刊投稿，接收日期为2025年9月24日，早期访问日期为2025年10月1日。期刊为MIC-ICT Research，卷号2025，期号3。论文可靠性较高，基于已建立的模型汤技术和CoAtNet架构，实验设计相对完整。可复现性等级为中等偏高，提供了详细的实验设置和评估协议，但缺少完整的开源代码。研究主题聚焦于文化遗产保护的计算机视觉应用，属于跨学科研究领域。论文引用了Wortsman等人关于模型汤的开创性工作，以及Dai等人关于CoAtNet的原始论文，理论基础扎实。研究机构为越南芹苴大学信息技术与通信学院，具有相关领域的研究背景。

## Evidence Anchors

1. claim: 模型汤技术在不增加推理成本的情况下提升性能
   - evidence: 论文明确指出模型汤是一种轻量级权重空间集成技术，通过平均单次训练轨迹的检查点而不增加推理成本
   - location: 摘要和3.3节
2. claim: CoAtNet架构采用C-C-T-T配置
   - evidence: 论文3.2节详细描述了CoAtNet的五阶段设计：S0为卷积茎，S1和S2使用MBConv块，S3和S4转换为Transformer块
   - location: 3.2节
3. claim: 模型汤在ICH-17数据集上达到72.36%准确率
   - evidence: 表4显示CoAtNet-2+均匀汤在测试集上的准确率为72.36%，F1分数为69.28%
   - location: 4.4.1节和表4
4. claim: k=8的选择基于经验验证
   - evidence: 论文4.2节明确说明选择k=8的原因：太少限制权重平均的潜在收益，太多可能引入噪声快照
   - location: 4.2节
5. claim: 模型汤选择几何多样性检查点
   - evidence: MDS可视化显示模型汤选择的成分在输出空间中广泛分布，而集成模型聚集在中心区域
   - location: 5节和图4
6. claim: 预训练对性能至关重要
   - evidence: 表7显示从零开始训练的CoAtNet-2准确率仅为51.27%，而预训练模型达到71.43%，相差约20个百分点
   - location: 6节和表7
7. claim: CoAtNet-0参数量约为ViT的三分之一但性能更好
   - evidence: 表3显示CoAtNet-0有26.68M参数，ViT有85.81M参数，但CoAtNet-0准确率达到70.63%优于ViT的70.09%
   - location: 4.4.1节和表3
8. claim: 模型汤通过减少方差提升泛化能力
   - evidence: 论文通过偏差-方差分解分析证明模型汤通过稳定不同模型快照的预测来减少方差
   - location: 摘要和5节
